title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The Great Migration in East Africa is one of the most spectacular wildlife events (0) on the planet, involving millions of wildebeest and zebras. (9) Although the journey is fraught with danger from predators and treacherous river crossings, the animals must move to find fresh grazing lands. (10) Such is the scale of this movement that it can be tracked by satellites orbiting high above the Earth. (11) Provided that the seasonal rains arrive on time, the vast herds follow a predictable clockwise route through the Serengeti ecosystem. (12) Not only does this migration sustain the local lion and hyena populations, but it also rejuvenates the grasslands through natural fertilization. (13) Regardless of the physical toll the journey takes, the instinct to survive drives these creatures across hundreds of miles. (14) Not until the late twentieth century did conservationists fully map the entire migratory path and the threats it faces. (15) Despite the increasing impact of human fences and climate change, the migration continues to be a vital pulse for the region. (16) Whether these ancient routes can be preserved for future generations is a question of global importance. This cycle of life remains a powerful symbol of nature’s enduring resilience.

example.text: The Great Migration in East Africa is one of the most spectacular wildlife events (0) on the planet.
example.explanation: Preposition used to indicate location relative to the Earth.

questions.explanation: Conjunction introducing a contrast or concession.
Determiner used for emphasis at the start of the clause.
Conjunction used to express a condition.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' structure.
Part of the fixed linker 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure triggering inversion ('did conservationists... map').
Preposition used to introduce a contrast.
Conjunction introducing two possibilities or a doubt.
